summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording of a stellar spring‑90 show with blowing‑away Blow Away, killer Just A Little Light and a soaring Victim → China Cat transition.
review: The audience (AUD) tape is remarkably clean – vocals and instruments are well separated and the crowd comes through, making it one of the better AUD sources. Musically the concert shines: Blow Away, Just A Little Light, Victim into China Cat, Morning Dew and a flawless Brokedown Palace encore are repeatedly praised, with only Dear Mr. Fantasy and a few post‑space numbers noted as weaker spots.

ai_rating.rationale: Three detailed reviews give uniformly high marks for both performance (standout jams, strong band interplay) and recording quality (clear, well‑balanced AUD), resulting in an overall rating near the top of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear vocals and instrument separation; audience audible without mudding the mix. No reported drop‑outs or major flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert opens strong, maintains momentum through a powerful second set, and delivers an uplifting post‑space segment. Minor dips in energy (Fantasy/Gimme Some Lovin') are outweighed by the high‑energy jams and seamless transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s solo on Blow Away is described as “best ever”; his tone shines throughout, especially on Victim, China Cat and the soaring Morning Dew. He also delivers a perfect Brokedown Palace encore.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is highlighted as “huge in the mix” during Victim/China Cat and generally powerful across the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Hammond work is praised – he never leaves it post‑space, drives the Real Love rap, and even stands up during Blow Away, adding to crowd excitement.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy (Bill) and Mickey are noted as “knew what they were doing,” providing solid rhythmic foundation throughout.
